name: Mutability of Python tuples vs lists
question_text: Which of the following statements about Python built-in types is correct?
general_feedback: Lists are mutable — their elements can be reassigned or appended in place. Tuples are immutable — once constructed, their elements cannot be rebound. (The objects referenced by a tuple can themselves be mutable, but the tuple slots are fixed.)

answers.text: Lists are mutable; tuples are immutable.
Lists are immutable; tuples are mutable.
answers.feedback: Reversed — list has append/extend/__setitem__; tuple does not.

answers.text: Both lists and tuples are immutable.
answers.feedback: Lists have in-place mutation via append, pop, and item assignment.

answers.text: Both lists and tuples are mutable.
answers.feedback: Tuple objects cannot be modified after construction.
